exitCodes.name: GenericError
exitCodes.description: Unclassified runtime failure (I/O error, bug, or captured panic).

exitCodes.name: StackNotFound
exitCodes.description: Stack, branch, or commit not found.

exitCodes.name: InvalidState
exitCodes.description: CLI invariant violated (e.g. command run outside a valid context).

exitCodes.name: ConfigurationError
exitCodes.description: Configuration file missing, unparseable, or failing validation.

command.name: mergify
command.longAbout:
Mergify command-line interface.

Drive Mergify from your terminal. Validate and simulate your configuration, manage and inspect the merge queue, schedule merge freezes, look at the tests tracked by CI Insights, and create and maintain stacked pull requests.

Most commands talk to the Mergify API and need a token. Set `MERGIFY_TOKEN` (or `GITHUB_TOKEN`) to apply it to every command, or pass `--token` to an individual command — there is no global `--token` on `mergify` itself. Run `mergify <command> --help` (or the git-style `mergify help <command>`) for detailed help and options on any command.

args.help: Increase log verbosity: -v info, -vv debug, -vvv trace. Logs go to stderr so stdout stays clean for piping. `RUST_LOG` overrides this
args.longHelp: Increase log verbosity: -v info, -vv debug, -vvv trace. Logs go to stderr so stdout stays clean for piping. `RUST_LOG` overrides this

args.help: Shorthand for at least debug-level logging (like -vv)
args.longHelp: Shorthand for at least debug-level logging (like -vv)

args.help: When to use color in terminal output
args.longHelp: When to use color in terminal output

commands.name: config
commands.about: Validate and simulate your Mergify configuration
commands.longAbout:
Validate and simulate your Mergify configuration.

Check your `.mergify.yml` against the schema before pushing it, or simulate how Mergify's rules would evaluate against a given pull request using your local configuration.

args.help: Path to the Mergify configuration file (auto-detected if not provided)
args.longHelp: Path to the Mergify configuration file (auto-detected if not provided)

commands.name: validate
commands.about: Validate the Mergify configuration file against the schema
commands.longAbout:
Validate the Mergify configuration file against the schema.

Check that your configuration file parses and conforms to the Mergify schema, reporting the first error with its location. Run it locally or in CI to catch mistakes before they reach the default branch. The file is auto-detected unless you pass `--config-file`.

commands.name: simulate
commands.about: Simulate Mergify actions on a pull request using the local configuration
commands.longAbout:
Simulate Mergify actions on a pull request using the local configuration.

Evaluate your local configuration against a real pull request and report which rules match and what actions Mergify would take — without changing anything on GitHub. Useful to test a configuration change before pushing it.

args.help: Pull request URL (e.g. <https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123>)
args.longHelp: Pull request URL (e.g. <https://github.com/owner/repo/pull/123>)

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

commands.name: ci
commands.about: Run Mergify CI Insights commands from your pipeline
commands.longAbout:
Run Mergify CI Insights commands from your pipeline.

Helpers meant to run inside a CI job: upload test reports, detect the build's git references, report the merge queue batch the build belongs to, and compute the scopes impacted by the changed files.

commands.name: scopes-send
commands.about: Send scopes tied to a pull request to Mergify
commands.longAbout:
Send scopes tied to a pull request to Mergify.

Upload the scopes affected by a pull request so CI Insights can attribute test results to them. Reads scopes from repeated --scope flags or from a file produced by "mergify ci scopes --write". Exits 0 without doing anything when no pull request can be determined.

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Pull request number. When omitted, it's detected from the CI environment: under GitHub Actions from the event payload (``.pull_request.number`` in ``GITHUB_EVENT_PATH``), under Buildkite from ``BUILDKITE_PULL_REQUEST``. When none can be detected the command prints a skip message and exits 0
args.longHelp: Pull request number. When omitted, it's detected from the CI environment: under GitHub Actions from the event payload (``.pull_request.number`` in ``GITHUB_EVENT_PATH``), under Buildkite from ``BUILDKITE_PULL_REQUEST``. When none can be detected the command prints a skip message and exits 0

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Scope to upload (repeatable)
args.longHelp: Scope to upload (repeatable)

args.help: JSON file containing scopes to upload (output of ``mergify ci scopes --write``)
args.longHelp: JSON file containing scopes to upload (output of ``mergify ci scopes --write``)

args.help: Plain-text file with one scope per line
args.longHelp: Plain-text file with one scope per line

args.help: Declare that the pull request impacts every scope. The merge queue then treats it as a barrier: it is never batched or run in parallel with other pull requests. The concrete scopes are still sent alongside the flag
args.longHelp: Declare that the pull request impacts every scope. The merge queue then treats it as a barrier: it is never batched or run in parallel with other pull requests. The concrete scopes are still sent alongside the flag

commands.name: git-refs
commands.about: Print the base/head git references for the current build
commands.longAbout:
Print the base/head git references for the current build.

Detect and print the base and head git references for the build from the CI environment, as plain text, eval-friendly shell lines, or JSON. Useful for wiring later steps to the same refs Mergify computed.

args.help: Output format: `text` (default), `shell` for eval-friendly `MERGIFY_GIT_REFS_*` lines, or `json` for a single JSON object
args.longHelp: Output format: `text` (default), `shell` for eval-friendly `MERGIFY_GIT_REFS_*` lines, or `json` for a single JSON object

commands.name: queue-info
commands.about: Print the current build's merge queue batch metadata (from the Mergify git note)
commands.longAbout:
Print the current build's merge queue batch metadata (from the Mergify git note).

Read the Mergify git note attached to the current HEAD and print the merge queue batch the build belongs to. Needs only plain git and no token, so it works in any CI runner.

commands.name: scopes
commands.about: Give the list of scopes impacted by changed files
commands.longAbout:
Give the list of scopes impacted by changed files.

Compute the configured scopes impacted by the files changed between two git references, using your Mergify configuration. Print them, or write them to a file with --write for a later "mergify ci scopes-send".

args.help: Path to YAML config file. Falls back to the `MERGIFY_CONFIG_PATH` env var, then auto-detects `.mergify.yml`, `.mergify/config.yml`, or `.github/mergify.yml`
args.longHelp: Path to YAML config file. Falls back to the `MERGIFY_CONFIG_PATH` env var, then auto-detects `.mergify.yml`, `.mergify/config.yml`, or `.github/mergify.yml`

args.help: Base git reference to use to look for changed files
args.longHelp: Base git reference to use to look for changed files

args.help: Head git reference to use to look for changed files
args.longHelp: Head git reference to use to look for changed files

args.help: Write the detected scopes to a file (JSON, consumed by `ci scopes-send --scopes-json`)
args.longHelp: Write the detected scopes to a file (JSON, consumed by `ci scopes-send --scopes-json`)

commands.name: junit-process
commands.about: Upload JUnit XML reports and ignore failed tests with Mergify's CI Insights Quarantine
commands.longAbout:
Upload JUnit XML reports and ignore failed tests with Mergify's CI Insights Quarantine.

Parse one or more JUnit XML reports, upload the results to CI Insights, and reconcile them against the quarantine so known-flaky tests don't fail the build. Accepts file paths or glob patterns; pass the runner's exit code with --test-exit-code to detect silent failures.

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default (`https://api.mergify.com`)
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default (`https://api.mergify.com`)

args.help: CI Issues application key. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN``
args.longHelp: CI Issues application key. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN``

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Test framework label (e.g. `pytest`). Optional; passed as a span attribute
args.longHelp: Test framework label (e.g. `pytest`). Optional; passed as a span attribute

args.help: Test language label (e.g. `python`). Optional; passed as a span attribute
args.longHelp: Test language label (e.g. `python`). Optional; passed as a span attribute

args.help: Branch the quarantine API should look up tests on. Defaults to the PR base branch, or the head branch as a fallback
args.longHelp: Branch the quarantine API should look up tests on. Defaults to the PR base branch, or the head branch as a fallback

args.help: Exit code of the test runner. When this is non-zero but no failures appear in the JUnit report, the run is flagged as a silent failure. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TEST_EXIT_CODE``
args.longHelp: Exit code of the test runner. When this is non-zero but no failures appear in the JUnit report, the run is flagged as a silent failure. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TEST_EXIT_CODE``

args.help: JUnit XML files or glob patterns (e.g. `reports/**/*.xml`). At least one path or pattern is required
args.longHelp: JUnit XML files or glob patterns (e.g. `reports/**/*.xml`). At least one path or pattern is required

commands.name: junit-upload
commands.about: Upload JUnit XML reports (deprecated: use junit-process)
commands.longAbout:
Upload JUnit XML reports (deprecated: use junit-process).

Deprecated alias for junit-process, kept for backward compatibility. It runs the same processing and prints a deprecation warning; use junit-process instead.

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default (`https://api.mergify.com`)
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default (`https://api.mergify.com`)

args.help: CI Issues application key. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN``
args.longHelp: CI Issues application key. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN``

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Test framework label (e.g. `pytest`). Optional; passed as a span attribute
args.longHelp: Test framework label (e.g. `pytest`). Optional; passed as a span attribute

args.help: Test language label (e.g. `python`). Optional; passed as a span attribute
args.longHelp: Test language label (e.g. `python`). Optional; passed as a span attribute

args.help: Branch the quarantine API should look up tests on. Defaults to the PR base branch, or the head branch as a fallback
args.longHelp: Branch the quarantine API should look up tests on. Defaults to the PR base branch, or the head branch as a fallback

args.help: Exit code of the test runner. When this is non-zero but no failures appear in the JUnit report, the run is flagged as a silent failure. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TEST_EXIT_CODE``
args.longHelp: Exit code of the test runner. When this is non-zero but no failures appear in the JUnit report, the run is flagged as a silent failure. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TEST_EXIT_CODE``

args.help: JUnit XML files or glob patterns (e.g. `reports/**/*.xml`). At least one path or pattern is required
args.longHelp: JUnit XML files or glob patterns (e.g. `reports/**/*.xml`). At least one path or pattern is required

commands.name: tests
commands.about: Inspect the tests tracked by Mergify CI Insights
commands.longAbout:
Inspect the tests tracked by Mergify CI Insights.

Look up a test's health and flakiness metrics, and manage the quarantine that keeps known-flaky tests from failing the merge queue.

commands.name: show
commands.about: Look up tests by name and print their health and metrics
commands.longAbout:
Look up tests by name and print their health and metrics.

Search CI Insights for one or more tests (by exact name or glob) and report their flakiness, failure rate, and recent history. Pass `--json` for machine-readable output.

args.help: Test name(s) to look up. Glob patterns (`*`, `?`) are supported by the API
args.longHelp: Test name(s) to look up. Glob patterns (`*`, `?`) are supported by the API

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Restrict matches to the given pipeline name(s)
args.longHelp: Restrict matches to the given pipeline name(s)

args.help: Exclude matches from the given pipeline name(s)
args.longHelp: Exclude matches from the given pipeline name(s)

args.help: Restrict matches to the given job name(s)
args.longHelp: Restrict matches to the given job name(s)

args.help: Exclude matches from the given job name(s)
args.longHelp: Exclude matches from the given job name(s)

args.help: Maximum number of identities the search endpoint may return per page (1–100, server default is 10)
args.longHelp: Maximum number of identities the search endpoint may return per page (1–100, server default is 10)

args.help: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose
args.longHelp: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose

commands.name: quarantines
commands.about: Manage the CI Insights quarantine
commands.longAbout:
Manage the CI Insights quarantine.

Add, remove, inspect, and list the tests held in the CI Insights quarantine — the set of known-flaky tests whose failures are ignored so they don't block the merge queue.

commands.name: add
commands.about: Add a test to the CI Insights quarantine
commands.longAbout:
Add a test to the CI Insights quarantine.

Quarantine a test by its fully qualified name so its failures stop blocking the merge queue. A reason is required; scope it to a branch with `--branch`, or quarantine on all branches by default.

args.help: Fully qualified name of the test to quarantine
args.longHelp: Fully qualified name of the test to quarantine

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Reason recorded for quarantining the test
args.longHelp: Reason recorded for quarantining the test

args.help: Branch name or pattern to scope the quarantine to. Omit to quarantine on all branches
args.longHelp: Branch name or pattern to scope the quarantine to. Omit to quarantine on all branches

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose
args.longHelp: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose

commands.name: remove
commands.about: Remove a test from the CI Insights quarantine
commands.longAbout:
Remove a test from the CI Insights quarantine.

Take a test out of the quarantine so its results count again. Identify it by test name or by quarantine id.

args.help: Test to remove from quarantine: either its fully qualified name or the quarantine id (as printed by `tests quarantines add`)
args.longHelp: Test to remove from quarantine: either its fully qualified name or the quarantine id (as printed by `tests quarantines add`)

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose
args.longHelp: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose

commands.name: get
commands.about: Print a single quarantine by test name or id
commands.longAbout:
Print a single quarantine by test name or id.

Show the details of one quarantined test — its reason, branch scope, and when it was added. Identify it by test name or by quarantine id.

args.help: Quarantine to print: either the test's fully qualified name or the quarantine id (as printed by `tests quarantines add`)
args.longHelp: Quarantine to print: either the test's fully qualified name or the quarantine id (as printed by `tests quarantines add`)

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose
args.longHelp: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose

commands.name: list
commands.about: List the tests currently in the CI Insights quarantine
commands.longAbout:
List the tests currently in the CI Insights quarantine.

Print every test currently held in the quarantine for the repository. Pass `--json` for machine-readable output.

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Detected from the CI environment or the local git remote when omitted

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose
args.longHelp: Emit a single JSON document to stdout instead of human prose

commands.name: queue
commands.about: Inspect and control the Mergify merge queue
commands.longAbout:
Inspect and control the Mergify merge queue.

Check the status of queued pull requests, inspect a single pull request's queue state, and pause or resume merging for a repository.

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Falls back to ``GITHUB_REPOSITORY`` env var
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Falls back to ``GITHUB_REPOSITORY`` env var

commands.name: pause
commands.about: Pause the merge queue for the repository
commands.longAbout:
Pause the merge queue for the repository.

Stop the queue from merging any pull request until it is resumed — useful during an incident or release window. A reason is required and shown to your team; queued pull requests stay in place.

args.help: Reason for pausing the queue (max 255 characters)
args.longHelp: Reason for pausing the queue (max 255 characters)

args.help: Skip the confirmation prompt. Required in non-interactive sessions
args.longHelp: Skip the confirmation prompt. Required in non-interactive sessions

commands.name: unpause
commands.about: Unpause the merge queue for the repository
commands.longAbout:
Unpause the merge queue for the repository.

Resume merging after a pause. The queue picks up where it left off with the pull requests still queued.

commands.name: status
commands.about: Show merge queue status for the repository
commands.longAbout:
Show merge queue status for the repository.

List the pull requests currently in the queue with their position and state. Filter by branch with `--branch`, or pass `--json` for machine-readable output.

args.help: Filter the queue by branch name
args.longHelp: Filter the queue by branch name

args.help: Emit the raw API response as a single JSON document
args.longHelp: Emit the raw API response as a single JSON document

commands.name: show
commands.about: Show detailed state of a pull request in the merge queue
commands.longAbout:
Show detailed state of a pull request in the merge queue.

Report the full queue state of a single pull request: its checks, the conditions it still needs to satisfy, and why it is or isn't mergeable. Pass `--verbose` for the full checks table and conditions tree, or `--json` for the raw response.

args.help: Pull request number to inspect
args.longHelp: Pull request number to inspect

args.help: Emit the raw API response as a single JSON document
args.longHelp: Emit the raw API response as a single JSON document

commands.name: freeze
commands.about: Schedule and manage merge freezes
commands.longAbout:
Schedule and manage merge freezes.

Create, list, update, and delete freezes that temporarily stop the merge queue from merging — for release windows, incidents, or code freezes.

args.help: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars
args.longHelp: Mergify or GitHub token. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_TOKEN`` and then ``GITHUB_TOKEN`` env vars

args.help: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default
args.longHelp: Mergify API URL. Falls back to ``MERGIFY_API_URL`` env var, then to the default

args.help: Repository full name (owner/repo). Falls back to ``GITHUB_REPOSITORY`` env var
args.longHelp: Repository full name (owner/repo). Falls back to ``GITHUB_REPOSITORY`` env var

commands.name: list
commands.about: List scheduled freezes for a repository
commands.longAbout:
List scheduled freezes for a repository.

Show the freezes currently configured for the repository, with their name, schedule, and state. Pass `--json` for machine-readable output.

args.help: Emit the raw `scheduled_freezes` array as a single JSON document
args.longHelp: Emit the raw `scheduled_freezes` array as a single JSON document

commands.name: create
commands.about: Create a new scheduled freeze
commands.longAbout:
Create a new scheduled freeze.

Add a freeze that stops the merge queue from merging while it is active — for a release window, incident, or code freeze.

args.help: Reason for the freeze
args.longHelp: Reason for the freeze

args.help: IANA timezone name (e.g. ``Europe/Paris``, ``US/Eastern``). Defaults to the system timezone when omitted
args.longHelp: IANA timezone name (e.g. ``Europe/Paris``, ``US/Eastern``). Defaults to the system timezone when omitted

args.help: Matching condition (repeatable, e.g. `-c base=main`)
args.longHelp: Matching condition (repeatable, e.g. `-c base=main`)

args.help: Start time in ISO 8601 format (default: now)
args.longHelp: Start time in ISO 8601 format (default: now)

args.help: End time in ISO 8601 format (default: no end / emergency freeze)
args.longHelp: End time in ISO 8601 format (default: no end / emergency freeze)

args.help: Exclude condition (repeatable, e.g. `-e label=hotfix`)
args.longHelp: Exclude condition (repeatable, e.g. `-e label=hotfix`)

commands.name: update
commands.about: Update an existing scheduled freeze
commands.longAbout:
Update an existing scheduled freeze.

Change the settings of an existing freeze, identified by its ID. Only the fields you pass are changed; the rest are left as they are.

args.help: Freeze ID (UUID)
args.longHelp: Freeze ID (UUID)

args.help: Reason for the freeze
args.longHelp: Reason for the freeze

args.help: IANA timezone name
args.longHelp: IANA timezone name

args.help: Matching condition (repeatable, e.g. `-c base=main`). Passing the flag one or more times replaces the existing list with the values supplied. Omitting `-c` entirely leaves the stored list untouched
args.longHelp: Matching condition (repeatable, e.g. `-c base=main`). Passing the flag one or more times replaces the existing list with the values supplied. Omitting `-c` entirely leaves the stored list untouched

args.help: Start time in ISO 8601 format
args.longHelp: Start time in ISO 8601 format

args.help: End time in ISO 8601 format
args.longHelp: End time in ISO 8601 format

args.help: Exclude condition (repeatable)
args.longHelp: Exclude condition (repeatable)

commands.name: delete
commands.about: Delete a scheduled freeze
commands.longAbout:
Delete a scheduled freeze.

Remove a freeze by its ID. If the freeze is currently active a reason is required, and the queue resumes merging once it's gone.

args.help: Freeze ID (UUID)
args.longHelp: Freeze ID (UUID)

args.help: Reason for deleting the freeze (required if the freeze is currently active)
args.longHelp: Reason for deleting the freeze (required if the freeze is currently active)

commands.name: stack
commands.about: Create and maintain stacked pull requests
commands.longAbout:
Create and maintain stacked pull requests.

Manage a stack of dependent branches and their pull requests: create, push, sync, reorder, reword, squash, and check out stacks built on top of your trunk.

commands.name: new
commands.about: Create a new stack branch
commands.longAbout:
Create a new stack branch.

Create a branch that tracks your trunk and start a fresh stack on top of it. The new branch is checked out by default; pass `--no-checkout` to only create the ref and stay on the current branch.

args.help: Name of the new branch
args.longHelp: Name of the new branch

args.help: Base branch to fork from, formatted as `REMOTE/BRANCH` (e.g. `origin/main`). When omitted, the trunk is resolved from the current branch's tracking info or `refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`
args.longHelp: Base branch to fork from, formatted as `REMOTE/BRANCH` (e.g. `origin/main`). When omitted, the trunk is resolved from the current branch's tracking info or `refs/remotes/origin/HEAD`

args.help: Checkout the new branch after creation. This is the default. Pass `--no-checkout` to keep the current branch checked out
args.longHelp: Checkout the new branch after creation. This is the default. Pass `--no-checkout` to keep the current branch checked out

args.help: Leave the current branch checked out and just create the new branch ref
args.longHelp: Leave the current branch checked out and just create the new branch ref

commands.name: note
commands.about: Attach a note explaining why a commit was amended
commands.longAbout:
Attach a note explaining why a commit was amended.

Record a note on a stack commit describing why it changed; the note is surfaced when reviewing the stack. Edit it in your editor or pass `-m`, add to an existing note with `--append`, or clear it with `--remove`. Defaults to the commit at HEAD.

args.help: Target commit. Accepts a SHA prefix, a ref (`HEAD~1`, branch name, …), or a Change-Id prefix (resolved against the stack walk). Defaults to HEAD
args.longHelp: Target commit. Accepts a SHA prefix, a ref (`HEAD~1`, branch name, …), or a Change-Id prefix (resolved against the stack walk). Defaults to HEAD

args.help: Note message. If omitted, opens `$GIT_EDITOR` / `$VISUAL` / `$EDITOR` / `vi` on a tempfile
args.longHelp: Note message. If omitted, opens `$GIT_EDITOR` / `$VISUAL` / `$EDITOR` / `vi` on a tempfile

args.help: Append to an existing note instead of replacing
args.longHelp: Append to an existing note instead of replacing

args.help: Remove the note on the target commit. Mutually exclusive with `--message` and `--append`
args.longHelp: Remove the note on the target commit. Mutually exclusive with `--message` and `--append`

commands.name: edit
commands.about: Edit a commit in the stack
commands.longAbout:
Edit a commit in the stack.

Pause an interactive rebase on the target commit so you can amend it, then resume. Omit the commit to start a fully interactive rebase of the whole stack.

args.help: Commit to pause the rebase on. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix; omit for a fully interactive rebase
args.longHelp: Commit to pause the rebase on. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix; omit for a fully interactive rebase

commands.name: drop
commands.about: Drop commits from the stack
commands.longAbout:
Drop commits from the stack.

Remove one or more commits from the stack and rebase the commits above them down. Each accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix. Use `--dry-run` to preview the resulting order first.

args.help: Commits to drop. Each accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix
args.longHelp: Commits to drop. Each accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix

args.help: Show the plan without dropping
args.longHelp: Show the plan without dropping

commands.name: fixup
commands.about: Fold commits into their parent
commands.longAbout:
Fold commits into their parent.

Squash each given commit into the commit below it, discarding the folded commit's message (the parent's message is kept). Each accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: Commits to fold into their parent
args.longHelp: Commits to fold into their parent

args.help: Show the plan without rebasing
args.longHelp: Show the plan without rebasing

commands.name: reword
commands.about: Change a commit's message
commands.longAbout:
Change a commit's message.

Rewrite the message of a commit in the stack. Pass `-m` to set the new message inline, or omit it to edit the message in your editor. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: Commit to reword. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix
args.longHelp: Commit to reword. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix

args.help: New message. When omitted, `git rebase -i` pauses at the target and opens `$GIT_EDITOR`
args.longHelp: New message. When omitted, `git rebase -i` pauses at the target and opens `$GIT_EDITOR`

args.help: Show the plan without rebasing
args.longHelp: Show the plan without rebasing

commands.name: reorder
commands.about: Reorder the stack's commits
commands.longAbout:
Reorder the stack's commits.

Rebase the stack into the order you list. List every commit in the stack — all of them must appear, in the new order. Each accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: Every commit in the stack, in the order you want them rebased into. All stack commits must be listed
args.longHelp: Every commit in the stack, in the order you want them rebased into. All stack commits must be listed

args.help: Show the plan without reordering
args.longHelp: Show the plan without reordering

commands.name: move
commands.about: Move a commit within the stack
commands.longAbout:
Move a commit within the stack.

Move one commit to a new position in the stack: to the `first` or `last` slot, or `before`/`after` another commit (passed as TARGET). Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: Commit to move. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix
args.longHelp: Commit to move. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix

args.help: Where to put it: `first`, `last`, `before`, `after`
args.longHelp: Where to put it: `first`, `last`, `before`, `after`

args.help: Required when `position` is `before` or `after`
args.longHelp: Required when `position` is `before` or `after`

args.help: Show the plan without moving
args.longHelp: Show the plan without moving

commands.name: squash
commands.about: Squash commits into a target commit
commands.longAbout:
Squash commits into a target commit.

Fold one or more source commits into a target commit, reordering them adjacent to it first. Use the form `SRC... into TARGET`. Pass `-m` to set the combined message; otherwise the target's message is kept. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: `SRC1 SRC2 ... into TARGET` — must contain exactly one `into` token; everything before is a source, the single token after is the target
args.longHelp: `SRC1 SRC2 ... into TARGET` — must contain exactly one `into` token; everything before is a source, the single token after is the target

args.help: Final commit message (required to rename; otherwise the target's message is kept)
args.longHelp: Final commit message (required to rename; otherwise the target's message is kept)

args.help: Show the plan without rebasing
args.longHelp: Show the plan without rebasing

commands.name: checkout
commands.about: Check out a pull request stack from GitHub
commands.longAbout:
Check out a pull request stack from GitHub.

Fetch a stack of pull requests by name and create a local branch that tracks its leaf, so you can continue working on a stack created elsewhere (a teammate's, or your own from another machine).

args.help: Name of the stack to check out
args.longHelp: Name of the stack to check out

args.help: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user
args.longHelp: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user

args.help: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote
args.longHelp: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote

args.help: Local branch name. Defaults to the normalised NAME
args.longHelp: Local branch name. Defaults to the normalised NAME

args.help: Override the stack branch prefix
args.longHelp: Override the stack branch prefix

args.help: Show the plan without checking out
args.longHelp: Show the plan without checking out

args.help: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch
args.longHelp: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch

args.help: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)
args.longHelp: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)

commands.name: sync
commands.about: Sync the stack with its trunk
commands.longAbout:
Sync the stack with its trunk.

Fetch the latest trunk, drop commits whose pull request has already merged, and rebase the remaining stack on top. Use `--dry-run` to preview.

args.help: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user
args.longHelp: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user

args.help: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote
args.longHelp: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote

args.help: Override the stack branch prefix
args.longHelp: Override the stack branch prefix

args.help: Show the plan without changing anything
args.longHelp: Show the plan without changing anything

args.help: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch
args.longHelp: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch

args.help: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)
args.longHelp: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)

commands.name: push
commands.about: Push the stack and create or update its pull requests
commands.longAbout:
Push the stack and create or update its pull requests.

Walk the local stack, optionally rebase it on trunk, push each commit to its own branch, and create or update the matching pull request and its Depends-On chain on GitHub. Use `--dry-run` to preview the plan and the rebase decision without touching anything.

args.help: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user
args.longHelp: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user

args.help: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote
args.longHelp: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote

args.help: Override the stack branch prefix
args.longHelp: Override the stack branch prefix

args.help: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch
args.longHelp: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch

args.help: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)
args.longHelp: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)

args.help: Skip the rebase step. By default `stack push` rebases on trunk before pushing when there are no approvals to dismiss
args.longHelp: Skip the rebase step. By default `stack push` rebases on trunk before pushing when there are no approvals to dismiss

args.help: Force the rebase even when PRs are approved (the rebase will dismiss the reviews). Mutually exclusive with `--skip-rebase`
args.longHelp: Force the rebase even when PRs are approved (the rebase will dismiss the reviews). Mutually exclusive with `--skip-rebase`

args.help: Only push the bottom commit of the stack
args.longHelp: Only push the bottom commit of the stack

args.help: Dry-run: render the plan + the rebase decision and exit
args.longHelp: Dry-run: render the plan + the rebase decision and exit

args.help: Open new PRs as drafts. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-create-as-draft` (`false` when unset)
args.longHelp: Open new PRs as drafts. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-create-as-draft` (`false` when unset)

args.help: Don't rewrite the PR title + body from the commit message; only update the rendered Depends-On chain in the body. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-keep-pr-title-body` (`false` when unset)
args.longHelp: Don't rewrite the PR title + body from the commit message; only update the rendered Depends-On chain in the body. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-keep-pr-title-body` (`false` when unset)

args.help: Don't create new PRs; surface would-be-created ones in the plan instead
args.longHelp: Don't create new PRs; surface would-be-created ones in the plan instead

args.help: Suppress the revision-history sticky comment update. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-revision-history` (`true` when unset)
args.longHelp: Suppress the revision-history sticky comment update. Default falls back to git config `mergify-cli.stack-revision-history` (`true` when unset)

args.help: Pass `--no-verify` to `git push` (skips local pre-push hooks)
args.longHelp: Pass `--no-verify` to `git push` (skips local pre-push hooks)

commands.name: list
commands.about: List the stack's commits and their pull requests
commands.longAbout:
List the stack's commits and their pull requests.

Show each commit in the current stack alongside its pull request, CI, and review state. Pass `--verbose` for per-check and per-reviewer detail, or `--json` for machine-readable output.

args.help: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user
args.longHelp: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user

args.help: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote
args.longHelp: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote

args.help: Override the stack branch prefix
args.longHelp: Override the stack branch prefix

args.help: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch
args.longHelp: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch

args.help: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)
args.longHelp: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)

args.help: Emit machine-readable JSON
args.longHelp: Emit machine-readable JSON

commands.name: open
commands.about: Open a stack commit's pull request in the browser
commands.longAbout:
Open a stack commit's pull request in the browser.

Open the GitHub pull request for a commit in the stack in your default browser. Defaults to the commit at HEAD.

args.help: Commit whose pull request to open. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix. When omitted: on a terminal, pick interactively from the stack (type to fuzzy-filter, HEAD preselected); otherwise defaults to HEAD
args.longHelp: Commit whose pull request to open. Accepts a SHA prefix or a Change-Id prefix. When omitted: on a terminal, pick interactively from the stack (type to fuzzy-filter, HEAD preselected); otherwise defaults to HEAD

args.help: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user
args.longHelp: Author of the stack. Defaults to the token's user

args.help: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote
args.longHelp: `owner/repo`. Falls back to the URL of `--trunk`'s remote

args.help: Override the stack branch prefix
args.longHelp: Override the stack branch prefix

args.help: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch
args.longHelp: Target trunk as `REMOTE/BRANCH`. Defaults to the resolved trunk for the current branch

args.help: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)
args.longHelp: GitHub token (falls back to `MERGIFY_TOKEN` / `GITHUB_TOKEN` / `gh auth token`)

commands.name: hooks
commands.about: Show or install the stack git hooks
commands.longAbout:
Show or install the stack git hooks.

Report the status of the git hooks that keep `Change-Id` trailers on your commits (stacks rely on them to track commits across rebases). Pass `--setup` to install or upgrade them.

args.help: Install or upgrade hooks
args.longHelp: Install or upgrade hooks

args.help: Force reinstall wrappers (use with --setup)
args.longHelp: Force reinstall wrappers (use with --setup)

commands.name: setup
commands.about: Install the stack git hooks
commands.longAbout:
Install the stack git hooks.

Install or upgrade the git hooks that add `Change-Id` trailers to your commits. This is an alias for `stack hooks --setup`; pass `--check` to report status instead of installing.

args.help: Force reinstall of hook wrappers, even if user modified them
args.longHelp: Force reinstall of hook wrappers, even if user modified them

args.help: Check status only (use 'stack hooks' instead)
args.longHelp: Check status only (use 'stack hooks' instead)

commands.name: self-update
commands.about: Update mergify to the latest release
commands.longAbout:
Update mergify to the latest release.

Download the newest published binary, verify it against the release `SHA256SUMS`, and atomically replace the running executable. Uses the same artifact and checksum contract as the `curl | sh` installer.

args.help: Re-download and re-install even when the running binary already matches the latest release tag. Useful for repairing a corrupted install without bumping the version
args.longHelp: Re-download and re-install even when the running binary already matches the latest release tag. Useful for repairing a corrupted install without bumping the version

args.help: Print the current and latest release tags and exit without touching the binary
args.longHelp: Print the current and latest release tags and exit without touching the binary

commands.name: completions
commands.about: Print a shell completion script (e.g. `mergify completions zsh`)
commands.longAbout: Print a shell completion script (e.g. `mergify completions zsh`)

args.help: Shell to generate a completion script for
args.longHelp: Shell to generate a completion script for
